input.tenant_message: The water heater in my unit started making a really loud popping/rumbling noise yesterday and now there's a small puddle of water forming around the base of it. I noticed the water pressure from the hot tap also seems lower than usual. The unit is in the closet next to the bathroom. I attached a photo of the puddle and the unit. It looks pretty old honestly.

ai_output.recommended_action: Schedule same-day service to inspect the water heater — pooling water at the base combined with rumbling sounds indicates a likely tank failure or pressure relief valve issue, which can escalate to flooding or, in rare cases, a pressure-related rupture. Turn off the cold water supply to the heater as a precaution and assess whether the unit needs repair or full replacement.
